title: Approval Center y acciones dry-run desde UI

architectural_decision.rationale: FUNC-SPRINT-71 implements Approval Center and dry-run UI actions under ADR-0013 Web UI first, Sprint 64 UI/API threat model and the existing Fase B approval/policy architecture; it does not change the approved local API/UI boundary or introduce a new execution model.

tests: python -m pytest tests/test_api_security.py tests/test_api_local.py tests/test_api_contract.py tests/test_api_reports_traces.py tests/test_api_approvals_actions.py tests/test_web_ui_mvp.py tests/test_web_ui_report_trace_viewer.py tests/test_web_ui_approval_center.py tests/test_application_services.py tests/test_application_services_v2.py -q
pass_criteria: Approval Center lists pending/approved/denied/revoked approval requests through /api/v1/approvals endpoints only.
Approval request/approve/deny operations are exposed through ApplicationService and policy-bound API routes.
Action Launcher supports only safe dry-run actions: readiness, code-review and refactor-plan.
Critical actions such as patch apply, refactor execute, rollback execute, git push and deploy are blocked from UI/API without creating a bypass.
Every approval mutation produces an auditable ApplicationResponse and uses the existing local approval store.
Web UI does not import devpilot_core, does not read outputs/ directly and does not access .devpilot database directly.
README, runbook, backlog, OpenAPI, API contract, mapping, audit and manifest remain synchronized.
block_criteria: Block if Web UI can execute critical/destructive actions without approval.
Block if patch apply/refactor execute/rollback execute/git push/deploy are available from UI.
Block if approval_id or token-like values are logged or rendered unsafely.
Block if API bypasses ApplicationService or PolicyEngine for dry-run actions.
Block if UI imports Python/core modules or reads local filesystem paths directly.
risks: Approval Center is an initial local MVP, not a multi-user RBAC workflow.
Browser token handling remains local-session based and must not be treated as enterprise authentication.
Dry-run action launcher intentionally exposes a small allowlist; broader actions require future policy/approval UX.
Approval evidence is local and should evolve toward richer trace/report correlation in later sprints.
Human approval state depends on the local workspace store and should be backed up with the rest of .devpilot state.
next_sprint: FUNC-SPRINT-72 — Settings UI: workspace, providers y políticas locales
